generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance VedderElectronicSpeedController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electronic speed controller that converts desired speed commands (in volts) into motor control signals for the vehicle's propulsion system, interfaced with the PhysicalTwinEntity via ROS2"
]

instance SteeringServoActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering servo actuator controlled via JetsonOrinNano through ROS2 nodes to adjust the vehicle's steering angle, directly interfaced with the PhysicalTwinEntity"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution LiDAR sensor capturing point clouds for object detection and tracking in the vehicle's environment"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance IntelDeepsenseD435iCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "RGB-D depth camera providing visual perception and environmental mapping data"
    DTDFVocab:producedData RGBDData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Comprehensive sensor suite including wheel encoders, IMU, and onboard sensors measuring speed, steering angle, and acceleration"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateTelemetry
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Data Transmission
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
    base:desc "Serialized LiDAR point cloud data processed via DBSCAN clustering and Kalman filtering for object tracking, transmitted over Wi-Fi with 100ms latency requirement"
]

instance RGBDData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDeepsenseD435iCamera
    base:desc "RGB-D data streams captured from the depth camera, transmitted via ROS2 network communication"
]

instance VehicleStateTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
    base:desc "Real-time vehicle state data including speed, steering angle, and acceleration transmitted via ROS2 middleware"
]

instance DetectedObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, VehicleStateSensor
    base:desc "Processed object tracks containing position, velocity, and size data of surrounding objects, transmitted after DBSCAN clustering and Kalman filtering"
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "DBSCAN algorithm implementation for LiDAR point cloud clustering and preprocessing"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware, MATLABSimulink
]

instance ObjectTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Kalman filter implementation for object tracking based on processed LiDAR data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware, MATLABSimulink
]

instance ACCControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "ISO 15622-compliant adaptive cruise control logic for lead vehicle detection and speed adjustment"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware, MATLABSimulink
]

instance ThreeDVisualizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D rendering model for displaying object tracks and vehicle state in a virtual cockpit environment"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance RawSensorStreams : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Unprocessed raw data streams from LiDAR and camera sensors"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData, RGBDData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringModel, ObjectTrackingModel
]

instance ProcessedObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Filtered and tracked object data after DBSCAN clustering and Kalman filtering"
    DTDFVocab:fromData DetectedObjectTracks
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ACCControlModel, ThreeDVisualizationModel
]

instance VehicleStateTelemetryData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed vehicle state data including speed, steering angle, and acceleration"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateTelemetry
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ACCControlModel, ThreeDVisualizationModel
]

instance HistoricalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Timeseries data of object tracks and vehicle state stored in SQL database for reporting"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessedObjectTracks, VehicleStateTelemetryData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ReportGenerationService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2Middleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Robot Operating System 2 providing middleware communication and node lifecycle management across all entities"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService, ReportGenerationService, DataStorageService
]

instance NVIDIAJetpack50 : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetpack 5.0 software development environment for Jetson Orin Nano hardware"
    DTDFVocab:enables PhysicalTwinEntity, DataTranslationFE
]

instance MATLABSimulink : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink environment for complex system simulations including point cloud processing"
    DTDFVocab:enables PointCloudClusteringModel, ObjectTrackingModel, ACCControlModel
]

instance SQLDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SQL database system for storing historical timeseries data of object tracks and vehicle state"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStorageService
]

instance WiFiNetwork : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Wi-Fi network infrastructure enabling 100ms end-to-end latency communication between Physical Twin and Digital Twin entities"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTranslationFE, DataCollectionFE
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring service displaying vehicle state, object tracks, and projected driving path through 3D visualization UI"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVisualizationInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service handling ACC logic (lead vehicle detection and speed adjustment) to reduce computational load on constrained PT hardware"
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCControlAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Remote control service enabling enable/disable of ACC, desired speed adjustments, and emergency braking commands via Control Panel UI"
    DTDFVocab:provides RemoteControlAction
]

instance ReportGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service generating timeseries reports of object tracks and vehicle state data"
    DTDFVocab:provides GeneratedReportInsight
]

instance DataStorageService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service managing historical data storage in SQL database"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataPersistenceInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D visualization of real-time vehicle state (speed, steering angle), object tracks, and projected driving path"
]

instance ACCControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Lead vehicle detection and speed adjustment logic implemented according to ISO 15622 standards"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance RemoteControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote enable/disable of ACC system, desired speed adjustments, and emergency braking commands"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance GeneratedReportInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Timeseries reports containing historical data of object tracks and vehicle state"
]

instance DataPersistenceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Historical data storage and retrieval capability through SQL database"
]

// C12: Functional Entities
instance DataCollectionFE : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "ROS2 package responsible for collecting raw sensor data from Physical Twin and preparing it for transmission"
    base:isContainedIn PhysicalTwinEntity
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationFE
]

instance DataTranslationFE : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "ROS2 package serializing and translating data streams between Physical Twin and Digital Twin entities"
    base:isContainedIn PhysicalTwinEntity
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PresentationFE, ControllingFE
]

instance ControllingFE : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "ROS2 package implementing ACC control logic and receiving commands from Digital Twin Entity"
    base:isContainedIn PhysicalTwinEntity
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ACCControlModel
]

instance PresentationFE : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Frontend component providing 3D visualization of vehicle state and object tracks"
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinEntity
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThreeDVisualizationModel
]

instance UserInterfaceFE : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Control panel UI enabling remote management of ACC system parameters"
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinEntity
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RemoteManagementService
]

// C13: Entities
instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Entity [
    base:desc "Custom entity encapsulating PT-specific resources including sensors, actuators, and ROS2 packages for data collection and control"
    base:contains DataCollectionFE, DataTranslationFE, ControllingFE
    base:isContainedIn VehicleEnvironment
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment VehicleEnvironment
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Entity [
    base:desc "Cloud/edge-hosted entity implementing core DT services including visualization, computation offloading, and remote management"
    base:contains PresentationFE, UserInterfaceFE
    base:isContainedIn SoftwareDevelopmentEnvironment
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment SoftwareDevelopmentEnvironment
]

instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Entity [
    base:desc "Entity representing user interface components for interaction with the Digital Twin system"
    base:contains UserInterfaceFE
    base:isContainedIn SoftwareDevelopmentEnvironment
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment SoftwareDevelopmentEnvironment
]

// C14: Environments
instance VehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical environment where the autonomous vehicle operates, containing all sensing and acting components"
    base:contains HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelDeepsenseD435iCamera, VehicleStateSensor, VedderElectronicSpeedController, SteeringServoActuator
]

instance SoftwareDevelopmentEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Software development environment including all tooling and enablers used in the DT system"
    base:contains ROS2Middleware, MATLABSimulink, SQLDatabase, NVIDIAJetpack50, WiFiNetwork
]

instance JetsonOrinNano : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano hardware platform hosting the Physical Twin Entity"
    DTDFVocab:enables PhysicalTwinEntity
]

instance CloudEdgePlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cloud/edge platform hosting the Digital Twin Entity"
    DTDFVocab:enables DigitalTwinEntity
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) sends control commands back to the Physical Twin (PT) via **ROS2-to-Wi-Fi communication** using the following outputs: (1) **Desired speed commands** (converted to motor voltage signals via the **Actuation FE**) for ACC functionality, (2) **Emergency braking commands** (directly sent to the motor controller), and (3) **Steering adjustments** (via ROS2 nodes interfacing with the steering servo). The **Controlling FE** in the DT computes the ACC logic (ISO 15622-compliant for Performance Class I) and sends actuation signals to the PT, which executes them via the **Actuation FE** in the Physical Twin Entity."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time-scale is defined by:
  - **Latency requirement**: End-to-end communication latency between PT and DT must be **≤100 ms** (as per 3GPP TS 22.185 for V2V communication).
  - **Clock synchronization**: All components must share a synchronized clock with an accuracy of **±100 ms** to ensure temporal consistency in distributed control.
  - **Perception frequency**: Object tracking and vehicle state updates occur at a rate sufficient to support real-time ACC (exact frequency not specified, but constrained by the 100 ms latency requirement)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is structured as a **three-tier, entity-based system** with **Physical Twin Entity** (PT-hosted), **Digital Twin Entity** (cloud/edge-hosted), and **User Entity** (UI components). Communication flows as follows: (1) **PT → DT**: Sensor data (LiDAR, camera, vehicle state) via ROS2 → Wi-Fi → Data Translation FE → Digital Twin Entity; (2) **DT → PT**: Control commands (speed, steering, braking) via Wi-Fi → ROS2 → Actuation FE → PT actuators. The **Data Translation FE** serializes and aligns data formats across entities. The system is deployed on **heterogeneous hardware**: PT (Jetson Orin Nano), DT (cloud/edge server), and UI (local/remote workstation)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) supports **operational and development lifecycle stages** of the Physical Twin (PT), primarily focusing on the **as-operated phase** (real-time monitoring, control, and optimization). It also aids in **design validation** (e.g., ACC logic testing) and **post-operation analysis** (e.g., report generation for performance review). The DT does not explicitly model the **as-designed** or **as-manufactured** phases but serves as a **cyber-physical experimentation facility** for continuous improvement of the PT’s ADAS systems."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The DT’s fidelity is ensured through **real-time sensor data synchronization** (100 ms latency), **Kalman-filter-based object tracking** (reducing noise in LiDAR data), and **ISO 15622-compliant ACC logic** (validated for Performance Class I). Validity mechanisms include: (1) **ROS2-based middleware** for deterministic communication, (2) **SQL database logging** for data integrity verification, (3) **3D visualization alignment** with on-board camera feeds (Figure 3), and (4) **latency testing** against 3GPP TS 22.185 requirements. Uncertainty sources include **sensor noise** (LiDAR/camera) and **clock drift** (mitigated via 100 ms synchronization). No explicit uncertainty quantification methods (e.g., Monte Carlo simulations) are mentioned in the documents."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT and Physical Twin (PT) communicate via a **Wi-Fi network** using **ROS2** for intra-entity communication and **custom UDP/TCP protocols** (serialized via **Data Translation FE**) for inter-entity data exchange. The **end-to-end latency** is constrained to **≤100 ms** (as per 3GPP TS 22.185 for V2V communication). Clock synchronization is achieved via **hardware-based timing alignment** (100 ms accuracy) to ensure temporal consistency for control loops. The **Physical Twin Entity** (ROS2 nodes on Jetson Orin Nano) interfaces directly with sensors/actuators, while the **Digital Twin Entity** (cloud/edge) communicates via Wi-Fi."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) is **partially hosted on the Physical Twin (PT)** (Physical Twin Entity on Jetson Orin Nano) and **partially deployed on a cloud/edge server** (Digital Twin Entity). The **User Entity** (UI components) can be deployed on **local workstations** (for developers) or **remote servers** (for operators). The **ROS2 network** is used for intra-PT communication, while **Wi-Fi** facilitates inter-PT/DT communication. The DT’s **Data Storage** component is hosted on an **external SQL database**, ensuring scalability for large datasets. No specific cloud provider (e.g., AWS, Azure) is mentioned, but the architecture supports cloud deployment."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The DT supports **limited horizontal integration** with external systems as described in the documents. No explicit mention of integration with **other DTs**, **ERP systems**, or **supply chain platforms** is provided. However, the architecture could theoretically integrate with: (1) **Vehicle Telematics Systems** (for fleet management), (2) **Cloud-Based ADAS Platforms** (for over-the-air updates), or (3) **Manufacturing DTs** (via ISO 23247-compliant data exchange). The **Data Translation FE** could facilitate interoperability with external standards (e.g., OPC UA)."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "The documents do not explicitly address **data ownership** or **privacy considerations**. However, the DT **stores ego vehicle state data externally** (SQL database) and enables **remote management**, implying potential **third-party access** (e.g., developers, operators). The **Physical Twin (PT)** is owned by the system developer (McMaster University), but the **DT data ownership** is ambiguous. **Privacy risks** may arise from transmitting **real-time sensor data** (e.g., LiDAR, camera feeds) over Wi-Fi, though no encryption or access control mechanisms are specified. The documents do not mention compliance with **GDPR, ISO 27001, or automotive cybersecurity standards** (e.g., AUTOSAR)."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT adheres to the following **standards**: (1) **ISO 23247** (Digital Twin Manufacturing Framework, Part 2: Reference Architecture) for the **entity-based design** and **Functional Entities (FEs)**; (2) **ISO 15622** (Road Vehicles — Adaptive Cruise Control Systems) for **ACC logic compliance** (Performance Class I); (3) **3GPP TS 22.185** for **V2V communication latency requirements** (≤100 ms); (4) **IEC 62541 (OPC UA)** (potential future use for data translation, though not implemented in this case); and (5) **ROS2** (Robot Operating System 2) for middleware communication. The architecture deviates from ISO 23247 by introducing a **custom Physical Twin Entity** and splitting the **User Interface FE** into visualization and control components."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The DT incorporates **safety and security considerations** aligned with automotive cyber-physical systems: (1) **Latency Constraints**: ≤100 ms end-to-end delay to prevent **control loop failures**; (2) **Clock Synchronization**: 100 ms accuracy to ensure **temporal consistency** in distributed control; (3) **Remote Management**: Emergency braking commands are directly sent to the PT, requiring **fail-safe mechanisms** (e.g., hardware-level overrides); (4) **Data Integrity**: ROS2 middleware ensures **deterministic communication** for critical control paths; (5) **Safety-Critical ACC Logic**: Compliance with **ISO 15622** for Performance Class I ACC. However, the documents do not address: (a) **cybersecurity threats** (e.g., Wi-Fi eavesdropping, command injection), (b) **physical safety** (e.g., fail-safe braking in case of DT failure), (c) **access control** (e.g., authentication for remote management), or (d) **compliance with automotive cybersecurity standards** (e.g., AUTOSAR, ISO/SAE 21434). The DT operates under a **Human-in-the-Loop** paradigm (David and Bork taxonomy) with **fully autonomous control** but no explicit redundancy or failover protocols."
]
